Instance.BindInstances: buff_0_0_U buff_0_1_U buff_1_0_U buff_1_1_U sub_fu_402_p2 add_ln129_fu_419_p2 j_2_fu_425_p2 add_ln133_fu_534_p2 add_ln141_fu_569_p2 add_ln142_fu_593_p2 r_1_fu_635_p2 c_1_fu_641_p2 add_ln135_fu_476_p2
dout_c_U input_stream_U iter_c25_U reorder_out_U iter_c24_U fft1_out_U iter_c23_U twiddle_out0_U twiddle_out1_U iter_c21_U iter_c22_U fft2_out0_U iter_c20_U fft2_out1_U joiner_out_U iter_c_U

field.name: Enable
field.description: Master enable for the device interrupt output to the system interrupt controller: 0 = Disabled, 1 = Enabled

field.name: CHAN0_INT_ST
field.description: Channel 0 (ap_done) Interrupt Status. 0 = No Channel 0 interrupt, 1 = Channel 0 interrupt.

field.name: CHAN1_INT_ST
field.description: Channel 1 (ap_ready) Interrupt Status. 0 = No Channel 1 interrupt, 1 = Channel 1 interrupt.

table.keys: Interface, Data Width (SW->HW), Address Width, Latency, Offset, Register, Max Widen Bitwidth, Max Read Burst Length, Max Write Burst Length, Num Read Outstanding, Num Write Outstanding, Resource Estimate
Interface, Data Width, Address Width, Offset, Register
Interface, Register, Offset, Width, Access, Description, Bit Fields
table.column: CTRL, 0x00, 32, RW, Control signals, 0=AP_START 1=AP_DONE 2=AP_IDLE 3=AP_READY 7=AUTO_RESTART 9=INTERRUPT
GIER, 0x04, 32, RW, Global Interrupt Enable Register, 0=Enable
IP_IER, 0x08, 32, RW, IP Interrupt Enable Register, 0=CHAN0_INT_EN 1=CHAN1_INT_EN
IP_ISR, 0x0c, 32, RW, IP Interrupt Status Register, 0=CHAN0_INT_ST 1=CHAN1_INT_ST
din_1, 0x10, 32, W, Data signal of din,
din_2, 0x14, 32, W, Data signal of din,
dout_1, 0x1c, 32, W, Data signal of dout,
dout_2, 0x20, 32, W, Data signal of dout,
iter, 0x28, 32, W, Data signal of iter,
clock, ap_clk
reset, ap_rst_n
interrupt, interrupt
ap_ctrl_hs,
in, ap_int<64>*
out, ap_int<64>*
in, int

section.name: M_AXI Burst Information
section.HEADER_NOTE: Note: All burst requests might be further partitioned into multiple requests during RTL generation based on max_read_burst_length or max_write_burst_length settings.

table.keys: HW Interface, Direction, Length, Width, Loop, Loop Location

table.keys: HW Interface, Variable, Access Location, Direction, Burst Status, Length, Loop, Loop Location, Resolution, Problem
table.column: din, fft_4k_ssr2_i16_c16_t16.cpp:66:34, read, Widen Fail, , VITIS_LOOP_61_1, fft_4k_ssr2_i16_c16_t16.cpp:61:22, 214-234, Sequential access length is not divisible by 2
din, fft_4k_ssr2_i16_c16_t16.cpp:66:34, read, Inferred, variable, VITIS_LOOP_61_1, fft_4k_ssr2_i16_c16_t16.cpp:61:22, ,
dout, fft_4k_ssr2_i16_c16_t16.cpp:100:15, write, Widen Fail, , VITIS_LOOP_90_1, fft_4k_ssr2_i16_c16_t16.cpp:90:22, 214-234, Sequential access length is not divisible by 2
dout, fft_4k_ssr2_i16_c16_t16.cpp:100:15, write, Inferred, variable, VITIS_LOOP_90_1, fft_4k_ssr2_i16_c16_t16.cpp:90:22, ,

Msg.msg_body: Since the only kind of statements allowed in a canonical dataflow region are variable declarations and function calls, the compiler may not be able to correctly handle the region

Pragma.options: mode=m_axi port=dout bundle=gmem2 depth=sim_depth max_widen_bitwidth=512 offset=slave
mode=m_axi port=din bundle=gmem1 depth=sim_depth max_widen_bitwidth=512 offset=slave
